issues.title: Test execution failed with exit code 1
issues.detail: The cargo test command exited with code 1, indicating test failure. No stdout excerpt was provided, making root cause analysis impossible. The stderr contains only compilation artifacts, suggesting the failure occurred during test runtime after successful build.

issues.title: Empty stdout_excerpt prevents result verification
issues.detail: Cannot verify any of the 7 expected success criteria: (1) Dilithium signature verification, (2) nonce acceptance, (3) expiry acceptance, (4) SR_0 computation, (5) lock_id issuance, (6) L1 transaction submission, (7) assertion pass/fail status. The test runner must capture and provide stdout to diagnose failure.

issues.title: Test panicked or assertion failed without captured output
issues.detail: Given exit code 1 and empty stdout, the test likely panicked, had an unhandled error, or encountered a database/connection issue. SQLX_OFFLINE=true suggests offline mode but no indication of sqlx::query errors or schema mismatches.

issues.title: SQLX_OFFLINE mode with no visible database errors
issues.detail: While SQLX_OFFLINE=true is set, there's no stderr indication of schema validation failures or missing .sqlx metadata files, which would typically appear. This suggests either the failure is upstream of database checks or error output was truncated.

issues.title: No lock record found
issues.detail: Query returned zero rows. Expected exactly one row with status=locked and pk_dilithium length=1952. This indicates either the locks table is empty, the consumer lock creation failed, or the lock was not persisted to the database.

observations: cast exited with code 1
RPC endpoint rpc.sepolia.org returned HTTP 522 (Connection timed out)
Cloudflare error page indicates origin web server timeout
No contract data retrieved; unable to evaluate expected condition

issues.title: RPC Infrastructure Outage
issues.detail: The L1 RPC endpoint (rpc.sepolia.org via Cloudflare) is unavailable. HTTP 522 error indicates the connection between Cloudflare and the origin server timed out. This is an infrastructure issue, not a contract or code regression.
